name: Set-PackageSource

options.name: -ConfigFile
options.description: Specifies a configuration file.

options.name: -Credential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to install package providers.

options.name: -Force
options.description: Forces the command to run without asking for user confirmation.

options.name: -ForceBootstrap
options.description: Indicates that `Set-PackageSource` forces PackageManagement to automatically install the package provider.

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Specifies a package source ID object that represents the package that you want to change. Package source IDs are part of the results of the `Get-PackageSource` cmdlet.

options.name: -Location
options.description: Specifies the current package source location. The value can be a URI, a file path, or any other destination format supported by the package provider.

name: -Name
-SourceName
options.description: Specifies a package source's name.

options.name: -NewLocation
options.description: Specifies the new location for a package source. The value can be a URI, a file path, or any other destination format supported by the package provider.

options.name: -NewName
options.description: Specifies the new name you assign to a package source.

options.name: -PackageManagementProvider
options.description: Specifies a package management provider.

name: -ProviderName
-Provider
options.description: Specifies a provider name.

options.name: -Proxy
options.description: Specifies a proxy server for the request, rather than connecting directly to the Internet resource.

options.name: -ProxyCredential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to use the proxy server that is specified by the Proxy parameter.

options.name: -PublishLocation
options.description: Specifies the publish location.

options.name: -ScriptPublishLocation
options.description: Specifies the script publish location.

options.name: -ScriptSourceLocation
options.description: Specifies the script source location.

options.name: -SkipValidate
options.description: Switch that skips validating the credentials of a package source.

options.name: -Trusted
options.description: Indicates that the source is a trusted package provider. Trusted sources don't prompt for verification to install packages.

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

description: Replaces a package source for a specified package provider.
